commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real-behavior proof and a small parser correction before normal maintainer review; automation cannot supply the contributor's setup proof.; Needs attention: The patch narrows exec capability, but the command-execution hardening is incomplete because one valid npm global update syntax bypasses the new guard.; Review finding: [P2] Handle npm --location global updates — src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts:1... (sha=7dfe3100317c4fc4793e1b150065c5491b8fcf91)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an exec preflight guard and tests to reject raw global npm/pnpm/bun/yarn installs or updates of the `openclaw` package, plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the exec preflight has no raw OpenClaw package-manager guard, while the linked maintainer issue includes concrete Ubuntu journal evidence for `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest` followed by stale runtime chunk import failures.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof and a small parser correction before normal maintainer review; automation cannot supply the contributor's setup proof.
